title: Bloodlines and Old Hatred
description: On pages 13–14, this part follows Unknown person #13, Unknown person #14 as Elbaf’s history turns darker as older conflicts, powerful bloodlines, and the roots of hatred behind the present crisis come into focus.
scene: Elbaf’s history turns darker as older conflicts, powerful bloodlines, and the roots of hatred behind the present crisis come into focus.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Damn!! My hands are tied right now!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Can you hear that? They're right outside!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But I'm afraid.. they're just a little too late!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Saint summers! Garling won't forgive this kind of transgression...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ZIP it! If you keep quiet, they'll just chalk it up to an accident!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Look at her face!!! ALL hope snuffed out!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: SEE! THIS AINT A FAIRY TALE! I'd just gut any hero who'd show up!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Life's a bitch, ain't it?! gHA ha!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'LL KILL YOU IN FIVE SLASHES! SAVOR THE PAIN!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Your wishful thinking will drain out with your blood!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll send you to the darkest shadows of the under- world.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Come and make me yours. - Shakuyaku

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm sorry... for letting it come to this.

summary: This part advances Elbaf’s Violent Past through 33 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
